⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇪🇬 🇵🇰 🇹🇷 🇮🇷 WSJ : Negotiators are pessimistic that Iran will meet U.S. President Trump’s Tuesday 8pm Eastern deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, paving the way for U.S. strikes on Iranian bridges and power plants. Iranian officials have told mediators they expect the U.S. and Israel to continue attacks even if negotiations move forward, given past patterns of setting deadlines followed by military action. U.S. Vice President JD Vance and envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are involved in daily talks. Egypt, Turkey and Pakistan are delivering messages between the sides. Decisions in Tehran are being run through the Supreme National Security Council and the new IRGC chief, Ahmed Vahidi. @wfwitness

One of General Atomics’ YFQ-42A drone test aircraft crashed shortly after takeoff Monday in the California desert. The company has paused test flights for now, is inspecting the aircraft, and is investigating the cause before resuming operations. It says this is too early to speculate.
